verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचएकैकशस्ता: कृष्णस्य पुत्रान् दश दशाबला: ।अजीजनन्ननवमान्पितु: सर्वात्मसम्पदा ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaekaikaśas tāḥ kṛṣṇasyaputrān daśa-daśābaāḥajījanann anavamānpituḥ sarvātma-sampadā
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Each of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s wives gave birth to ten sons, who were not less than their father, having all His personal opulence.

verses.sanskrit: गृहादनपगं वीक्ष्य राजपुत्र्योऽच्युतं स्थितम् ।प्रेष्ठं न्यमंसत स्वं स्वं न तत्तत्त्वविद: स्‍त्रिय: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: gṛhād anapagaṁ vīkṣyarāja-putryo ’cyutaṁ sthitampreṣṭhaṁ nyamaṁsata svaṁ svaṁna tat-tattva-vidaḥ striyaḥ
verses.text: Because each of these princesses saw that Lord Acyuta never left her palace, each thought herself the Lord’s favorite. These women did not understand the full truth about Him.

verses.sanskrit: चार्वब्जकोशवदनायतबाहुनेत्र-सप्रेमहासरसवीक्षितवल्गुजल्पै: ।सम्मोहिता भगवतो न मनो विजेतुंस्वैर्विभ्रमै: समशकन् वनिता विभूम्न: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: cārv-abja-kośa-vadanāyata-bāhu-netra-sa-prema-hāsa-rasa-vīkṣita-valgu-jalpaiḥsammohitā bhagavato na mano vijetuṁsvair vibhramaiḥ samaśakan vanitā vibhūmnaḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Lord’s wives were fully enchanted by His lovely, lotuslike face, His long arms and large eyes, His loving glances imbued with laughter, and His charming talks with them. But with all their charms these ladies could not conquer the mind of the all-powerful Lord.

verses.sanskrit: स्मायावलोकलवदर्शितभावहारि-भ्रूमण्डलप्रहितसौरतमन्त्रशौण्डै: ।पत्न्‍यस्तु षोडशसहस्रमनङ्गबाणै-र्यस्येन्द्रियं विमथितुं करणैर्न शेकु: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: smāyāvaloka-lava-darśita-bhāva-hāribhrū-maṇḍala-prahita-saurata-mantra-śauṇḍaiḥpatnyas tu śoḍaśa-sahasram anaṅga-bāṇairyasyendriyaṁ vimathitum karaṇair na śekuḥ
verses.text: The arched eyebrows of these sixteen thousand queens enchantingly expressed those ladies’ secret intentions through coyly smiling sidelong glances. Thus their eyebrows boldly sent forth conjugal messages. Yet even with these arrows of Cupid, and with other means as well, they could not agitate Lord Kṛṣṇa’s senses.

verses.sanskrit: इत्थं रमापतिमवाप्य पतिं स्‍त्रियस्ताब्रह्मादयोऽपि न विदु: पदवीं यदीयाम् ।भेजुर्मुदाविरतमेधितयानुराग-हासावलोकनवसङ्गमलालसाद्यम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: itthaṁ ramā-patim avāpya patiṁ striyas tābrahmādayo ’pi na viduḥ padavīṁ yadīyāmbhejur mudāviratam edhitayānurāga-hāsāvaloka-nava-saṅgama-lālasādyam
verses.text: Thus these women obtained as their husband the master of the goddess of fortune, although even great demigods like Brahmā do not know how to approach Him. With ever-increasing pleasure, they felt loving attraction for Him, exchanged smiling glances with Him, eagerly anticipated associating with Him in ever-fresh intimacy and enjoyed in many other ways.

verses.sanskrit: प्रत्युद्गमासनवरार्हणपादशौच-ताम्बूलविश्रमणवीजनगन्धमाल्यै: ।केशप्रसारशयनस्‍नपनोपहार्यै-र्दासीशता अपि विभोर्विदधु: स्म दास्यम् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: pratyudgamāsana-varārhaṇa-pāda-śauca-tāmbūla-viśramaṇa-vījana-gandha-mālyaiḥkeśa-prasāra-śayana-snapanopahāryaiḥdāsī-śatā api vibhor vidadhuḥ sma dāsyam
verses.text: Although the Supreme Lord’s queens each had hundreds of maidservants, they chose to personally serve the Lord by approaching Him humbly, offering Him a seat, worshiping Him with excellent paraphernalia, bathing and massaging His feet, giving Him pān to chew, fanning Him, anointing Him with fragrant sandalwood paste, adorning Him with flower garlands, dressing His hair, arranging His bed, bathing Him and presenting Him with various gifts.

verses.sanskrit: तासां या दशपुत्राणां कृष्णस्‍त्रीणां पुरोदिता: ।अष्टौ महिष्यस्तत्पुत्रान् प्रद्युम्नादीन् गृणामि ते ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāsāṁ yā daśa-putrāṇāṁkṛṣṇa-strīṇāṁ puroditāḥaṣṭau mahiṣyas tat-putrānpradyumnādīn gṛṇāmi te
verses.text: Among Lord Kṛṣṇa’s wives, each of whom had ten sons, I previously mentioned eight principal queens. I shall now recite for you the names of those eight queens’ sons, headed by Pradyumna.

verses.sanskrit: चारुदेष्ण: सुदेष्णश्च चारुदेहश्च वीर्यवान् ।सुचारुश्चारुगुप्तश्च भद्रचारुस्तथापर: ॥ ८ ॥चारुचन्द्रो विचारुश्च चारुश्च दशमो हरे: ।प्रद्युम्नप्रमुखा जाता रुक्‍मिण्यां नावमा: पितु: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: cārudeṣṇaḥ sudeṣṇaś cacārudehaś ca vīryavānsucāruś cāruguptaś cabhadracārus tathāparaḥ
verses.text: The first son of Queen Rukmiṇī was Pradyumna, and also born of her were Cārudeṣṇa, Sudeṣṇa and the powerful Cārudeha, along with Sucāru, Cārugupta, Bhadracāru, Cārucandra, Vicāru and Cāru, the tenth. None of these sons of Lord Hari was less than his father.

verses.sanskrit: भानु: सुभानु: स्वर्भानु: प्रभानुर्भानुमांस्तथा ।चन्द्रभानुर्बृहद्भ‍ानुरतिभानुस्तथाष्टम: ॥ १० ॥श्रीभानु: प्रतिभानुश्च सत्यभामात्मजा दश ।साम्ब: सुमित्र: पुरुजिच्छतजिच्च सहस्रजित् ॥ ११ ॥विजयश्चित्रकेतुश्च वसुमान् द्रविड: क्रतु: ।जाम्बवत्या: सुता ह्येते साम्बाद्या: पितृसम्मता: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhānuḥ subhānuḥ svarbhānuḥprabhānur bhānumāṁs tathācandrabhānur bṛhadbhānuratibhānus tathāṣṭamaḥ
verses.text: The ten sons of Satyabhāmā were Bhānu, Subhānu, Svarbhānu, Prabhānu, Bhānumān, Candrabhānu, Bṛhadbhānu, Atibhānu (the eighth), Śrībhānu and Pratibhānu. Sāmba, Sumitra, Purujit, Śatajit, Sahasrajit, Vijaya, Citraketu, Vasumān, Draviḍa and Kratu were the sons of Jāmbavatī. These ten, headed by Sāmba, were their father’s favorites.

verses.sanskrit: वीरश्चन्द्रोऽश्वसेनश्च चित्रगुर्वेगवान् वृष: ।आम: शङ्कुर्वसु: श्रीमान् कुन्तिर्नाग्नजिते: सुता: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: vīraś candro ’śvasenaś cacitragur vegavān vṛṣaḥāmaḥ śaṅkur vasuḥ śrīmānkuntir nāgnajiteḥ sutāḥ
verses.text: The sons of Nāgnajitī were Vīra, Candra, Aśvasena, Citragu, Vegavān, Vṛṣa, Āma, Śaṅku, Vasu and the opulent Kunti.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत: कविर्वृषो वीर: सुबाहुर्भद्र एकल: ।शान्तिर्दर्श: पूर्णमास: कालिन्द्या: सोमकोऽवर: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutaḥ kavir vṛṣo vīraḥsubāhur bhadra ekalaḥśāntir darśaḥ pūrṇamāsaḥkālindyāḥ somako ’varaḥ
verses.text: Śruta, Kavi, Vṛṣa, Vīra, Subāhu, Bhadra, Śānti, Darśa and Pūrṇamāsa were sons of Kālindī. Her youngest son was Somaka.

verses.sanskrit: प्रघोषो गात्रवान्सिंहो बल: प्रबल ऊर्धग: ।माद्रय‍ा: पुत्रा महाशक्ति: सह ओजोऽपराजित: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: praghoṣo gātravān siṁhobalaḥ prabala ūrdhagaḥmādryāḥ putrā mahāśaktiḥsaha ojo ’parājitaḥ
verses.text: Mādrā’s sons were Praghoṣa, Gātravān, Siṁha, Bala, Prabala, Ūrdhaga, Mahāśakti, Saha, Oja and Aparājita.

verses.sanskrit: वृको हर्षोऽनिलो गृध्रो वर्धनोन्नाद एव च ।महांस: पावनो वह्निर्मित्रविन्दात्मजा: क्षुधि: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: vṛko harṣo ’nilo gṛdhrovardhanonnāda eva camahāṁsaḥ pāvano vahnirmitravindātmajāḥ kṣudhiḥ
verses.text: Mitravindā’s sons were Vṛka, Harṣa, Anila, Gṛdhra, Vardhana, Unnāda, Mahāṁsa, Pāvana, Vahni and Kṣudhi.

verses.sanskrit: सङ्ग्रामजिद् बृहत्सेन: शूर: प्रहरणोऽरिजित् ।जय: सुभद्रो भद्राया वाम आयुश्च सत्यक: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: saṅgrāmajid bṛhatsenaḥśūraḥ praharaṇo ’rijitjayaḥ subhadro bhadrāyāvāma āyuś ca satyakaḥ
verses.text: Saṅgrāmajit, Bṛhatsena, Śūra, Praharaṇa, Arijit, Jaya and Subhadra were the sons of Bhadrā, together with Vāma, Āyur and Satyaka.

verses.sanskrit: दीप्तिमांस्ताम्रतप्ताद्या रोहिण्यास्तनया हरे: ।प्रद्यम्नाच्चानिरुद्धोऽभूद्रुक्‍मवत्यां महाबल: ।पुत्र्यां तु रुक्‍मिणो राजन् नाम्ना भोजकटे पुरे ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: dīptimāṁs tāmrataptādyārohiṇyās tanayā hareḥpradyamnāc cāniruddho ’bhūdrukmavatyāṁ mahā-balaḥputryāṁ tu rukmiṇo rājannāmnā bhojakaṭe pure
verses.text: Dīptimān, Tāmratapta and others were the sons of Lord Kṛṣṇa and Rohiṇī. Lord Kṛṣṇa’s son Pradyumna fathered the greatly powerful Aniruddha in the womb of Rukmavatī, the daughter of Rukmī. O King, this took place while they were living in the city of Bhojakaṭa.

verses.sanskrit: एतेषां पुत्रपौत्राश्च बभूवु: कोटिशो नृप ।मातर: कृष्णजातीनां सहस्राणि च षोडश ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: eteṣāṁ putra-pautrāś cababhūvuḥ koṭiśo nṛpamātaraḥ kṛṣṇa-jātīnāṁsahasrāṇi ca ṣoḍaśa
verses.text: My dear King, the sons and grandsons of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s children numbered in the tens of millions. Sixteen thousand mothers gave rise to this dynasty.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीराजोवाचकथं रुक्‍म्यरीपुत्राय प्रादाद् दुहितरं युधि ।कृष्णेन परिभूतस्तं हन्तुं रन्ध्रं प्रतीक्षते ।एतदाख्याहि मे विद्वन् द्विषोर्वैवाहिकं मिथ: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-rājovācakathaṁ rukmy arī-putrāyaprādād duhitaraṁ yudhikṛṣṇena paribhūtas taṁhantuṁ randhraṁ pratīkṣateetad ākhyāhi me vidvandviṣor vaivāhikaṁ mithaḥ
verses.text: King Parīkṣit said: How could Rukmī give his daughter to his enemy’s son? After all, Rukmī had been defeated by Lord Kṛṣṇa in battle and was waiting for an opportunity to kill Him. Please explain this to me, O learned one — how these two inimical parties became united through marriage.

verses.sanskrit: अनागतमतीतं च वर्तमानमतीन्द्रियम् ।विप्रकृष्टं व्यवहितं सम्यक् पश्यन्ति योगिन: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: anāgatam atītaṁ cavartamānam atīndriyamviprakṛṣṭaṁ vyavahitaṁsamyak paśyanti yoginaḥ
verses.text: Mystic yogīs can perfectly see that which has not yet happened, as well as things in the past or present, beyond the senses, remote or blocked by physical obstacles.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचवृत: स्वयंवरे साक्षादनङ्गोऽङ्गयुतस्तया ।राज्ञ: समेतान् निर्जित्य जहारैकरथो युधि ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācavṛtaḥ svayaṁvare sākṣādanaṇgo ’ṇga-yutas tayārājñaḥ sametān nirjityajahāraika-ratho yudhi
verses.text: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: At her svayaṁvara ceremony, Rukmavatī herself chose Pradyumna, who was the re-embodiment of Cupid. Then, although He fought alone on a single chariot, Pradyumna defeated the assembled kings in battle and took her away.

verses.sanskrit: यद्यप्यनुस्मरन् वैरं रुक्‍मी कृष्णावमानित: ।व्यतरद् भागिनेयाय सुतां कुर्वन् स्वसु: प्रियम् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: yady apy anusmaran vairaṁrukmī kṛṣṇāvamānitaḥvyatarad bhāgineyāyasutāṁ kurvan svasuḥ priyam
verses.text: Though Rukmī always remembered his enmity toward Lord Kṛṣṇa, who had insulted him, in order to please his sister he sanctioned his daughter’s marriage to his nephew.

verses.sanskrit: रुक्‍मिण्यास्तनयां राजन् कृतवर्मसुतो बली ।उपयेमे विशालाक्षीं कन्यां चारुमतीं किल ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: rukmiṇyās tanayāṁ rājankṛtavarma-suto balīupayeme viśālākṣīṁkanyāṁ cārumatīṁ kila
verses.text: O King, Balī, the son of Kṛtavarmā, married Rukmiṇī’s young daughter, large-eyed Cārumatī.

verses.sanskrit: दौहित्रायानिरुद्धाय पौत्रीं रुक्‍म्याददाद्धरे: ।रोचनां बद्धवैरोऽपि स्वसु: प्रियचिकीर्षया ।जानन्नधर्मं तद् यौनं स्‍नेहपाशानुबन्धन: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: dauhitrāyāniruddhāyapautrīṁ rukmy ādadād dhareḥrocanāṁ baddha-vairo ’pisvasuḥ priya-cikīrṣayājānann adharmaṁ tad yaunaṁsneha-pāśānubandhanaḥ
verses.text: Rukmī gave his granddaughter Rocanā to his daughter’s son, Aniruddha, despite Rukmī’s relentless feud with Lord Hari. Although Rukmī considered this marriage irreligious, he wanted to please his sister, bound as he was by the ropes of affection.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन्नभ्युदये राजन् रुक्‍मिणी रामकेशवौ ।पुरं भोजकटं जग्मु: साम्बप्रद्युम्नकादय: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasminn abhyudaye rājanrukmiṇī rāma-keśavaupuraṁ bhojakaṭaṁ jagmuḥsāmba-pradyumnakādayaḥ
verses.text: On the joyous occasion of that marriage, O King, Queen Rukmiṇī, Lord Balarāma, Lord Kṛṣṇa and several of the Lord’s sons, headed by Sāmba and Pradyumna, went to the city of Bhojakaṭa.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन् निवृत्त उद्वाहे कालिङ्गप्रमुखा नृपा: ।द‍ृप्तास्ते रुक्‍मिणं प्रोचुर्बलमक्षैर्विनिर्जय ॥ २७ ॥अनक्षज्ञो ह्ययं राजन्नपि तद्‌व्यसनं महत् ।इत्युक्तो बलमाहूय तेनाक्षैर्रुक्‍म्यदीव्यत ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmin nivṛtta udvāhekāliṅga-pramukhā nṛpāḥdṛptās te rukmiṇaṁ procurbalam akṣair vinirjaya
verses.text: After the wedding, a group of arrogant kings headed by the King of Kaliṅga told Rukmī, “You should defeat Balarāma at dice. He’s not expert at dice, O King, but still He’s quite addicted to it.” Thus advised, Rukmī challenged Balarāma and began a gambling match with Him.

verses.sanskrit: शतं सहस्रमयुतं रामस्तत्राददे पणम् ।तं तु रुक्‍म्यजयत्तत्र कालिङ्ग: प्राहसद् बलम् ।दन्तान् सन्दर्शयन्नुच्चैर्नामृष्यत्तद्धलायुध: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śataṁ sahasram ayutaṁrāmas tatrādade paṇamtaṁ tu rukmy ajayat tatrakāliṅgaḥ prāhasad balamdantān sandarśayann uccairnāmṛṣyat tad dhalāyudhaḥ
verses.text: In that match Lord Balarāma first accepted a wager of one hundred coins, then one thousand, then ten thousand. Rukmī won this first round, and the King of Kaliṅga laughed loudly at Lord Balarāma, showing all his teeth. Lord Balarāma could not tolerate this.

verses.sanskrit: ततो लक्षं रुक्‍म्यगृह्णाद्‌ग्लहं तत्राजयद् बल: ।जितवानहमित्याह रुक्‍मी कैतवमाश्रित: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: tato lakṣaṁ rukmy agṛhṇādglahaṁ tatrājayad balaḥjitavān aham ity āharukmī kaitavam āśritaḥ
verses.text: Next Rukmī accepted a bet of one hundred thousand coins, which Lord Balarāma won. But Rukmī tried to cheat, declaring “I’m the winner!”

verses.sanskrit: मन्युना क्षुभित: श्रीमान् समुद्र इव पर्वणि ।जात्यारुणाक्षोऽतिरुषा न्यर्बुदं ग्लहमाददे ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: manyunā kṣubhitaḥ śrīmānsamudra iva parvaṇijātyāruṇākṣo ’ti-ruṣānyarbudaṁ glaham ādade
verses.text: Shaking with anger like the ocean on the full-moon day, handsome Lord Balarāma, His naturally reddish eyes even redder in His fury, accepted a wager of one hundred million gold coins.

verses.sanskrit: तं चापि जितवान् रामो धर्मेण छलमाश्रित: ।रुक्‍मी जितं मयात्रेमे वदन्तु प्राश्न‍िका इति ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ cāpi jitavān rāmodharmeṇa chalam āśritaḥrukmī jitaṁ mayātremevadantu prāśnikā iti
verses.text: Lord Balarāma fairly won this wager also, but Rukmī again resorted to cheating and declared, “I have won! Let these witnesses here say what they saw.”

verses.sanskrit: तदाब्रवीन्नभोवाणी बलेनैव जितो ग्लह: ।धर्मतो वचनेनैव रुक्‍मी वदति वै मृषा ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tadābravīn nabho-vāṇībalenaiva jito glahaḥdharmato vacanenaivarukmī vadati vai mṛṣā
verses.text: Just then a voice from the sky declared, “Balarāma has fairly won this wager. Rukmī is surely lying.”

verses.sanskrit: तामनाद‍ृत्य वैदर्भो दुष्टराजन्यचोदित: ।सङ्कर्षणं परिहसन् बभाषे कालचोदित: ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tām anādṛtya vaidarbhoduṣṭa-rājanya-coditaḥsaṅkarṣaṇaṁ parihasanbabhāṣe kāla-coditaḥ
verses.text: Urged on by the wicked kings, Rukmī ignored the divine voice. In fact destiny itself was urging Rukmī on, and thus he ridiculed Lord Balarāma as follows.

verses.sanskrit: नैवाक्षकोविदा यूयं गोपाला वनगोचरा: ।अक्षैर्दीव्यन्ति राजानो बाणैश्च न भवाद‍ृशा: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: naivākṣa-kovidā yūyaṁgopālā vana-gocarāḥakṣair dīvyanti rājānobāṇaiś ca na bhavādṛśāḥ
verses.text: [Rukmī said:] You cowherds who wander about the forests know nothing about dice. Playing with dice and sporting with arrows are only for kings, not for the likes of You.

verses.sanskrit: रुक्‍मिणैवमधिक्षिप्तो राजभिश्चोपहासित: ।क्रुद्ध: परिघमुद्यम्य जघ्ने तं नृम्णसंसदि ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: rukmiṇaivam adhikṣiptorājabhiś copahāsitaḥkruddhaḥ parigham udyamyajaghne taṁ nṛmṇa-saṁsadi
verses.text: Thus insulted by Rukmī and ridiculed by the kings, Lord Balarāma was provoked to anger. In the midst of the auspicious wedding assembly, He raised His club and struck Rukmī dead.

verses.sanskrit: कलिङ्गराजं तरसा गृहीत्वा दशमे पदे ।दन्तानपातयत् क्रुद्धो योऽहसद् विवृतैर्द्विजै: ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaliṅga-rājaṁ tarasāgṛhītvā daśame padedantān apātayat kruddhoyo ’hasad vivṛtair dvijaiḥ
verses.text: The King of Kaliṅga, who had laughed at Lord Balarāma and shown his teeth, tried to run away, but the furious Lord quickly seized him on his tenth step and knocked out all his teeth.

verses.sanskrit: अन्ये निर्भिन्नबाहूरुशिरसो रुधिरोक्षिता: ।राजानो दुद्रवर्भीता बलेन परिघार्दिता: ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: anye nirbhinna-bāhūru-śiraso rudhirokṣitāḥrājāno dudravar bhītābalena paṅghārditāḥ
verses.text: Tormented by Lord Balarāma’s club, the other kings fled in fear, their arms, thighs and heads broken and their bodies drenched in blood.

verses.sanskrit: निहते रुक्‍मिणि श्याले नाब्रवीत् साध्वसाधु वा ।रक्‍मिणीबलयो राजन् स्‍नेहभङ्गभयाद्धरि: ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: nihate rukmiṇi śyālenābravīt sādhv asādhu vārakmiṇī-balayo rājansneha-bhaṅga-bhayād dhariḥ
verses.text: When His brother-in-law Rukmī was slain, Lord Kṛṣṇa neither applauded nor protested, O King, for He feared jeopardizing His affectionate ties with either Rukmiṇī or Balarāma.

verses.sanskrit: ततोऽनिरुद्धं सह सूर्यया वरंरथं समारोप्य ययु: कुशस्थलीम् ।रामादयो भोजकटाद् दशार्हा:सिद्धाखिलार्था मधुसूदनाश्रया: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: tato ’niruddhaṁ saha sūryayā varaṁrathaṁ samāropya yayuḥ kuśasthalīmrāmādayo bhojakaṭād daśārhāḥsiddhākhilārthā madhusūdanāśrayāḥ
verses.text: Then the descendants of Daśārha, headed by Lord Balarāma, seated Aniruddha and His bride on a fine chariot and set off from Bhojakaṭa for Dvārakā. Having taken shelter of Lord Madhusūdana, they had fulfilled all their purposes.
